title: atalantia scandens
intro: Atalantia scandens, also known as the wild orange or Malay orange, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory infections. Atalantia scandens can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
